plan_adherence_notes: All 6 plan steps were executed correctly. Steps 1-5 produced the right test code; step 6 confirmed all tests pass. The plan miscounted existing tests as 9 (actual: 11) and planned for 10 new tests but 11 were added (negative currency was split into 2 separate it-blocks instead of 1). This is a minor improvement, not a deviation. Fake timer pattern, negative sign preservation, custom truncateHash length, and non-sha256 truncation all match the plan's decisions d1-d3. All 22 tests pass.
missed_questions: What are the boundary behaviors of formatRelativeTime at exactly 60 minutes and exactly 24 hours?
What does timeUntil return for a diff of exactly 0 milliseconds (boundary between expired and not)?
How does formatCurrency handle 0 and very small negative numbers (e.g., -0.5)?
Does truncateHash behave correctly when len exceeds the hash body length?
reason: Execution faithfully followed the plan. All 22 tests (11 existing + 11 new) pass. The new tests cover formatRelativeTime (4 cases: just now, minutes, hours, days), timeUntil (3 cases: expired, hours+minutes, days+hours), negative formatCurrency (2 cases: -K, -M), and truncateHash edge cases (2 cases: custom length, non-sha256). Research was thorough with specific code references and all assumptions were verified correct. The missed questions are edge-case boundaries that go beyond the task's specified test cases and are not blocking.
